title: Rested, Tender Egyptian Sweet Sherry Wrap
description: A salty, tender, brunch-mood Egyptian dish featuring sweet sherry, nuka-pickle, and pandan-leaf wraps. Perfect for a FODMAP-friendly breakfast or brunch.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Fleur de sel or other flaky salt is preferred.

instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Brush the pandan-leaf wraps with olive oil and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until slightly charred.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon sweet sherry over low heat.
Add the canned tuna and cook, stirring occasionally, until heated through.
Mix in the nuka-pickled cucumber, chopped parsley, mint, salt, and black pepper.
Spoon the tuna mixture onto the center of each pandan-leaf wrap.
Drizzle the remaining sweet sherry over the top and sprinkle with chopped cilantro and dill.
Fold the bottom edge of the wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrap to enclose.
Serve immediately, with feta cheese on top if desired.
Garnish with additional parsley or mint if desired.
Serve with a side of lemon wedges, if desired.
Serve hot or at room temperature.
